telegram.title: Qatar supplies 40% of India’s LNG, much of it routed through the Strait of Hormuz. Any disruption there doesn’t just hit global markets, but it hits India’s power, fertiliser and city gas lifelines almost immediately.
telegram.india_relevance: India relies on Qatar for nearly half of its natural gas imports, making the Strait of Hormuz a critical chokepoint for domestic energy security.
telegram.why_it_matters:
A disruption here triggers a domino effect across Indian power generation and fertilizer production, likely necessitating expensive spot-market purchases.
It highlights India's vulnerability to Middle Eastern geopolitical volatility despite efforts to diversify energy sources.
